latest.title: Appointment of Chief Revenue Officer
latest.content:
26 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1669Y
dotDigital Group plc
26 March 2026
26 March 2026
Dotdigital Group plc
("Dotdigital" or the "Group")
Appointment of Chief Revenue Officer
Dotdigital Group (AIM: DOTD), the leading SaaS provider of an AI-powered customer experience and data platform for intelligent personalised marketing engagement at scale, is pleased to announce the appointment of Mike Harris as Chief Revenue Officer (non-board position), effective 1 April 2026.
The Chief Revenue Officer role is a newly created Group position, established to bring all global go
‑
to
‑
market functions together under unified leadership as the Group continues to scale internationally. The role reports directly to the Chief Executive Officer and is responsible for leading Dotdigital's global commercial organisation and revenue performance, with a focus on driving more consistent execution across regions and improving expansion, retention and customer lifetime value.
Mike has served as Dotdigital's Interim GM for EMEA since July 2025, during which time he has made a strong contribution to the Group's commercial strategy and revenue performance in the region.
Mike brings extensive senior leadership experience in scaling revenue across global B2B SaaS and marketing technology businesses. Most recently, he served as CRO at Haiilo, where he drove several growth, retention and efficiency-related initiatives. Prior to this, he held senior commercial leadership roles including Vice President of Global Sales at parcelLab and Senior Vice-President of Global Sales at Monetate, both marketing technology platforms. Mike has also held senior roles at Bluecore and Omniture.
Milan Patel, CEO of Dotdigital, commented:
"Dotdigital has strengthened significantly in recent years through sustained product investment and targeted acquisitions, building a broader, more capable platform. We will continue to invest as we extend that advantage, while at the same time focusing on raising the consistency and pace of execution globally. Creating the Chief Revenue Officer role gives us clear leadership of the Group's commercial engine and reinforces our ambition to scale internationally with discipline.
"Since joining us as interim GM for EMEA last summer, Mike has raised the bar on focus, pace and commercial discipline. I am delighted he will now lead this agenda as Chief Revenue Officer as we deepen customer success, drive expansion and retention, and deliver sustainable, high-quality recurring revenue growth."
Mike Harris, incoming CRO, commented:
"Over the past several months I have seen a platform with real strength, a loyal customer base, and a team that cares deeply about what we do - and we know that our success is ultimately defined by the success of our customers. As CRO, my priority is to help our teams focus on the right opportunities, make performance visible through clear metrics, and support customers to realise value faster. I am excited to take on the role and help drive the next stage of growth."
Contacts
Dotdigital Group plc
+44 (0)20 3953 3072
Milan Patel, CEO
investorrelations@dotdigital.com
Tom Mullan, CFO
Alma Strategic Communications
+44 (0)20 3405 0210
Hilary Buchanan
dotdigital@almastrategic.com
David Ison
Will Merison
Canaccord Genuity (Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited (Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7220 0500
Jonny Franklin-Adams, Corporate Finance
Sunila de Silva, Equity Capital Markets
Singer Capital Markets (Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7496 3000
Shaun Dobson
Jen Boorer
About Dotdigital
Dotdigital Group plc (AIM: DOTD) is a leading provider of cross-channel marketing automation technology to marketing professionals. Dotdigital's customer experience and data platform (CXDP) combines the power of automation and AI to help businesses deliver hyper-relevant customer experiences at scale. With Dotdigital, marketing teams can unify and enrich their customer data, identify valuable customer segments, and deliver personalised cross-channel customer journeys that result in engagements, conversions, and loyalty.
Founded in 1999, Dotdigital is headquartered in London with offices in Manchester, Southampton, New York, Melbourne, Sydney, Singapore, Tokyo, Warsaw and Cape Town. Dotdigital's solutions empower over 4,000 brands across 150 countries.

company_30s.what_they_do: Dotdigital delivers cloud‑based email marketing, marketing automation and customer data platforms that help businesses create personalised campaigns and measure customer journeys.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global email‑marketing market is worth tens of billions of pounds and is growing as brands shift spend from paid media to owned‑channel engagement. Dotdigital’s data‑driven personalization tools compete with the likes of HubSpot and Salesforce, positioning it in a high‑margin niche that is hard for new entrants to replicate.
company_30s.current_state: Dotdigital is in a pivot phase: it has just acquired the AI‑enabled Alia platform, appointed a new Chief Revenue Officer, and is reporting interim results that hint at a shift toward profitability. Cash burn remains a concern, but the leadership changes and new product line suggest a push to broaden revenue streams.

rns_narrative: The company is reorganising its leadership, bringing in a new CRO and making several director‑shareholder changes to align incentives with growth.

Operationally, Dotdigital has released a series of updates—interim results, a trading update, and a half‑year presentation—showing that the business is moving from cash‑burn to a more sustainable model.
The acquisition of Alia is the key catalyst, adding AI‑driven personalization capabilities that could unlock new customer segments and cross‑sell opportunities.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quietly pivoting – the crowd’s ears are closed, but the acquisition whispers a potential upside.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders praise the Alia deal as a growth lever, noting that it could double the value of the data platform.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears focus on AI competition, share dilution from the acquisition, and doubts about whether Dotdigital can monetize its expanded offering quickly enough.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has remained flat; the acquisition news sparked a minor uptick, but overall chatter is minimal.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no one is talking about the stock, creating a perfect surprise window if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly earnings release in May 2026 – a positive surprise could trigger a bounce.
Public announcement of the first Alia‑powered product launch, which would validate the acquisition.
Any new partnership or client win that demonstrates revenue traction.
A volume spike that breaks the 52‑week low support level, indicating fresh buying interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to confirm the thesis include: volume rising >2× the 20‑day average, price breaking the 52‑week low by at least 5%, and a clear earnings beat of at least 10%. Also keep an eye on any share‑dilution news that could erode value.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits deep in a crash (over 80% down). It appears to be in the early capitulation phase – the market has already sold most weak hands. A mean‑reversion is likely once a catalyst (earnings or the Alia integration) provides fresh conviction.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Deep crashes historically deliver 150–200% upside on average. If Dotdigital recovers to 50% of its all‑time high, that’s roughly a 250% gain from the current level. The recent acquisition and leadership overhaul give a plausible trigger for a reversal, and the lack of public sentiment means the market may be under‑priced.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Risks include the possibility that the AI integration fails to generate revenue, that the company continues to dilute shareholders, or that competitors outpace it in the personalization space. In the worst case, the stock could stay below 10% of its peak, leaving a 90% loss.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~250% if price reaches 50% of ATH. Potential downside: limited to the current price (no further drop beyond the 86% drawdown already taken). Risk‑reward ratio is roughly 1:2.5.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 52‑week low with volume >1.5× the 20‑day average. Use a 20% stop‑loss below the entry to protect against a false break. Allocate 10% of your discretionary capital to the position.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small protective position (or a short‑dated call) until you see a clear earnings beat or the first Alia‑powered product launch. Watch for a 2× volume spike and a 5% move above the low as the entry trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has surfaced; therefore no exit recommendation based on fundamental collapse.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Dotdigital is a deep‑crashed, trend‑dead play that could deliver a 250% upside once earnings or the Alia integration materialises, offering a high‑reward, low‑risk contrarian opportunity.

Historical data shows 1 rallies averaging 90% upside. Current position: +17.3%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
